nodes.summary: Full assembled knowledge graph from previous analysis run — contains all nodes, edges, layers, and tours merged from 9 batches. 11475 lines. Version 1.0.0 with project metadata (ccbot, Python, python-telegram-bot, tmux).

Batch 1 analysis output — file-level nodes for core source files (bot.py, config.py, callback_data.py, directory_browser.py, monitor_state.py, screenshot.py, session_monitor.py, transcribe.py). Each node includes summary, tags, complexity, and languageNotes.

Batch 2 analysis output — module/function/class/constant-level nodes for handlers (cleanup.py, interactive_ui.py, message_queue.py, message_sender.py, response_builder.py, status_polling.py) plus session/tmux modules. Fine-grained symbol extraction with line ranges.

Batch 3 analysis output — file-level nodes for message delivery pipeline (transcript_parser.py, telegram_sender.py, markdown_v2.py, response_builder.py, message_sender.py, history.py). Focus on MarkdownV2 conversion, transcript parsing, message splitting, and pagination.
Batch 4 analysis output — pipeline nodes for CI/CD workflows (check.yml, claude-code-review.yml, claude.yml). Includes edges for pipeline relationships: pr_pipeline, shared_action, ci_infrastructure.
Batch 5 analysis output — document nodes for .claude/rules architecture rule files (architecture.md, message-handling.md, topic-architecture.md). Captures key topics and architectural invariants.

Batch 6 analysis output — project/config/document/concept nodes for root-level files (ccbot_project, .env.example, pyproject.toml, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, README_CN.md, README_RU.md) plus architectural concepts (1-to-1 mapping, hook tracking, message queue, rate limiting, formatting, inline UI, state persistence, voice transcription).

Batch 7 analysis output — document nodes for font license files (LICENSE-JetBrainsMono.txt, LICENSE-NotoSansMono.txt, LICENSE-Symbola.txt) plus concept nodes tying font licenses to the OFL standard. Covers SIL Open Font License 1.1 terms and public-domain licensing.

Batch 8 analysis output — file nodes for remaining project files (.understandignore, doc files, scripts/restart.sh, src/ccbot/__init__.py, font binary, handlers/__init__.py, hook.py, main.py, all test files). 3981 lines. Covers infrastructure, documentation, and test file metadata.

Batch 9 analysis output — test file nodes with FilenameNode/ClassNode/FunctionNode types for test files (conftest.py, test_config_integration.py, test_monitor_state_integration.py). Detailed class hierarchies, methods, and test scenarios.

Batch orchestration metadata — schemaVersion 1, louvain algorithm, 66 total files across 9 batches. Contains exportsByPath mapping every analyzed file to its exported symbols (functions, classes, constants) for dependency tracking.

Final output graph — minimal version 1.0.0 manifest with project metadata (name: ccbot, languages: python/shell/markdown/yaml/toml, frameworks: python-telegram-bot/tmux). Contains only the project header without node/edge payload.
Input for file fingerprinting step — lists all 66 source file paths in the project with the project root path (D:\CCbot_tmux\ccbot\ccbot_ju1ian). Drives the initial file discovery phase of analysis.
Input for import scanning phase — lists 45 source files with language and fileCategory metadata. Drives the Python import dependency analysis to build the import map.

Import dependency map between source files — 45 files scanned, 15 with imports, 63 total edges. Maps each Python file to its imported dependencies (e.g., bot.py imports 18 modules). Core dependency graph for understanding module coupling.

Architectural layer definitions — 10 layers grouping nodes by concern: Bot Application, Message Processing, Session & State, Terminal & Tmux, Transcription, Handlers, Testing, Configuration, Documentation, Infrastructure. Maps file nodes to architectural tiers.

Quality review output — lists 116 orphan node warnings (nodes with no edges) across all types. Stats: 530 total nodes, 555 total edges, 10 layers, 8 tour steps. Node type breakdown: 40 files, 17 classes, 90 functions, 34 concepts, 292 symbols.

Guided tour definitions — 8 ordered tour steps guiding codebase exploration: Project Overview, Entry Point & Config, Bot Application Core, Session & State Management, Terminal & Tmux Integration, Message Processing Pipeline, Handler Layer, Infrastructure & Cross-Cutting Concerns. Each step maps to key file nodes.

edges.label: input_for
edges.content: import-map-input.json provides the source file list; import-map.json contains the analyzed dependency edges

edges.label: feeds_into
edges.content: Import dependency edges from import-map.json are incorporated into the assembled graph's edge set

edges.label: feeds_into
edges.content: fingerprint-input.json provides the master file list used during assembly of all nodes

edges.label: derives
edges.content: Architectural layers are derived from the assembled graph's node set by grouping by concern

edges.label: derives
edges.content: Quality review (orphan detection, stats) is computed from the assembled graph's nodes and edges

edges.label: derives
edges.content: Guided tour entries are authored based on the assembled graph's layered structure and key file nodes

edges.label: produces
edges.content: The final-graph.json output is a condensed version of the assembled graph with project metadata

edges.label: refines
edges.content: Review findings inform the final graph output; orphan node warnings identify gaps for future refinement

edges.label: orchestrates
edges.content: batches.json contains the exportsByPath metadata used during assembly to link file nodes to their exported symbols

edges.label: sequential_batch
edges.content: Batches 1 and 2 are sequential analysis passes; batch-1 covers file-level, batch-2 drills into symbol-level

edges.label: informs
edges.content: Tour step ordering follows the layered architecture from layers.json (Application → Processing → State → Terminal → Handlers → Infrastructure)
